description:
You are the on-call sysadmin and need to document that the loopback interface is healthy.

1. From the terminal, send exactly four (4) ICMP echo-requests to 127.0.0.1.
2. Derive the three required numeric values from the command’s statistics:
   • total packets transmitted  
   • total packets received  
   • packet-loss percentage  
3. Create the directory /home/user/network_logs  (if it does not already exist).
4. Inside that directory, create a new ASCII text file named loopback_ping.log whose contents **must be exactly three lines** in the order shown below, with no extra spaces or blank lines:

packets_transmitted=<number>
packets_received=<number>
packet_loss_percent=<percentage>

For a healthy loopback test the finished file should look like:

packets_transmitted=4
packets_received=4
packet_loss_percent=0%

The automated checker will:
• verify that the file /home/user/network_logs/loopback_ping.log exists  
• confirm it contains exactly three lines in the precise format shown above (all lowercase, key and value joined by a single “=” character).  
• ensure the numeric values reflect the actual ping statistics obtained in step 1.

Produce no other files. No root privileges are required. A single well-structured command (plus redirection/grepping or minimal text processing) is enough.
truth:
Expected artefact:
File path: /home/user/network_logs/loopback_ping.log  
Exact contents (including trailing newline at end of file):

packets_transmitted=4
packets_received=4
packet_loss_percent=0%

Only this file under /home/user/network_logs should be created or modified.
name: task_000000_21ea3013
